title: Weekly open incident summary
problem: Create a recurring summary of open incidents for operational leadership.
approach: Use a saved search, email template, and scheduled automation to deliver the report reliably.
outcome: Leadership gets consistent visibility without manual prep.

title: Customer response reactivation
problem: Reactivate waiting incidents when the customer responds.
approach: Detect the response event, update state, notify the owner, and preserve auditability.
outcome: Support work resumes faster and customer follow-up is not lost.

title: Multi-step request approvals
problem: Route requests through different approvers based on request category and risk.
approach: Use approval logic, branching, notifications, and fallback handling.

title: Change approval and scheduling
problem: Support governed changes with controlled approvals and execution windows.
approach: Combine change type, approval requirements, schedule checks, and communication controls.
outcome: Change work is better governed and lower risk.

title: Knowledge deflection for support
problem: Improve support efficiency by surfacing reusable articles during ticket handling.
approach: Tie incident trends and agent workflows to article creation and reuse.
outcome: Resolution becomes faster and repeat questions reduce.

title: External approval sync
problem: Keep Ivanti status synchronized with an external approval platform.
approach: Design a secure bidirectional integration with mapping, retries, and state updates.

title: Scheduled report push to external endpoint
problem: Push recurring operational data to another platform.
approach: Use scheduled extraction, mapping, validation, and retry/error monitoring.
outcome: External systems receive structured data on time.

title: Pilot-based patch rollout
problem: Deploy patches safely using pilot devices and phased rollout.
approach: Use patch rings, exception tracking, and success gates before broad rollout.
outcome: Risk is reduced while keeping progress measurable.

title: Targeted software rollout
problem: Deploy software only to intended devices and validate completion.
approach: Use carefully designed device groups, schedules, validation, and remediation.

title: BYOD enrollment program
problem: Set up a BYOD program that balances policy with privacy.
approach: Define ownership, enrollment path, app strategy, support guidance, and compliance behavior.
outcome: Users can enroll while the organization maintains governance.

title: Non-compliant device response
problem: Respond consistently when a managed device violates policy.
approach: Evaluate severity, notify the user, apply remediation/restriction, and track recovery.
outcome: Security response is consistent and measurable.

title: Managed mobile app rollout
problem: Roll out a business app to the right managed device groups.
approach: Target by ownership/compliance, monitor installs, and support exceptions.
outcome: Mobile rollout stays controlled and easier to support.

title: Asset refresh planning
problem: Identify aging assets for refresh planning and review.
approach: Use lifecycle states, reporting, and ownership visibility to surface candidates.

title: Discovery reconciliation improvement
problem: Improve trust where discovered inventory and asset records do not align.
approach: Normalize fields, review identifiers, resolve duplicates, and validate reporting.
outcome: Inventory trust improves for operations and audit.

title: Control assessment cycle
problem: Track an assessment from authority/citation through control review and corrective action.
approach: Map requirements, assess controls, capture gaps, and assign corrective actions.

title: Workspace shift-left triage
problem: Use unified analyst context and quick actions to reduce escalation volume.
approach: Surface relevant context, suggest action, and document resolution patterns.
outcome: Analysts resolve more at first touch.

title: Warehouse picking redesign
problem: Simplify a warehouse picking flow that currently has too many prompts.
approach: Reduce screens, make scanning primary, and optimize exception prompts.
outcome: Frontline users complete work faster with fewer errors.
